scope: deployed ELF and retained local artifact comparison
current rent-exempt minima for UsernameIndex and InviteIndex lengths
derived page continuity checks for the observed inviter
InviteIndex entry and current PlayerProfile observations
current Play entry and invite-spawn consumer boundary

network.rpcVersionBoundary: getVersion and getMultipleAccounts reported 4.2.0-beta.1 while getProgramAccounts context metadata reported 3.1.8. The audit records each method rather than treating them as one backend version.

playerProgram.lastDeployUtcBoundary: The RPC node had cleaned the historical block, so getBlockTime could not recover a reliable UTC timestamp.
playerProgram.artifactBoundary: The retained local artifact matches the deployed ELF bytes. Modified source files postdate or differ from that artifact and are not thereby proven to reproduce it.

rent.boundary: These are point-in-time rent-exempt minima, not transaction fees, refundable deposits, or proof that the current program exposes a close instruction.

playerProgramAccountInventory.driftFromOlderPlayerProfileCopy: The older page recorded 46 total accounts and 9 PlayerSessions. The InviteIndex count stayed at one; the extra current account is a PlayerSession.

methods: getMultipleAccounts for the discovered inviter's derived page indexes 0 through 63
getProgramAccounts prefix inventory for the complete Player Program
inviteIndexScan.scopeBoundary: The derived check is deeper than the current browser's sixteen-page scan but covers only the inviter discovered from the finalized program-account evidence.

observedInviteIndex.duplicateBoundary: No duplicate was observed in this two-entry snapshot. The current contract append routine does not scan for duplicates, so the snapshot is not evidence of protocol-level uniqueness.
observedInviteIndex.profileBoundary: Both observed wallets happened to have current PlayerProfiles at the recorded slot. InviteIndex stores no Profile or name and the append instruction does not require either.

currentRuntime.probeResult: At 2026-07-16T15:03:35Z, a fresh unsigned-browser recheck returned HTTP 200, retained the wallet-scoped pending-spawn sentinel after current Play startup, contained neither profileInviteOpen nor profileInvitePanel, exposed three canvases, and produced no console or page error.
currentRuntime.alternateConsumerPath: src/main.js imports and consumes the draft, but that module is not the current Play entry.
currentRuntime.boundary: Current Play starts from query pose, wallet-agnostic saved local position, or the Genesis default and later reads chain Profile data asynchronously without teleporting to it.

worldCapture.captureMethod: Headless Chromium with WebGL2 SwiftShader; a public non-signing placeholder session passed the route guard; UI siblings were hidden before the selected canvas capture.
worldCapture.boundary: This is a dated real Play scene capture. It does not prove invitation, pending-spawn persistence, chain position, or that the current runtime applied an invite Region.

scope: single consumption and malformed draft removal
current cleanup rethrow when storage read and removal both fail
focusedTests.boundary: These local tests document the current source behavior. They do not prove a deployed invitation transaction, browser persistence, or current Play consumption.

interpretationLimits: A URL referrer, Guardian ID, or Region is editable public text and is not a credential or inviter signature.

An accepted UsernameIndex proves program-validated name ownership under its stored fields; it does not reserve a missing account during a prior read and does not automatically prove the current Profile display name.
An InviteIndex entry proves that the invited wallet signed an append transaction for that inviter address. The inviter does not sign each append.

The current contract does not deduplicate wallets and permits high pages to be pre-created without checking the previous page. Normal contiguous paging is therefore a client convention, not a complete contract invariant.

The current browser scans sixteen pages and can display at most 1024 entries. It can append to page index 16 but cannot display it, and a full page index 16 prevents its current rollover logic from reaching index 17.
The invite page contains no display name, Guardian, Region, spawn coordinates, reward entitlement, token transfer, or local receipt.
The pending spawn draft has no expiry check and the current production Play entry does not consume it.
No current instruction closes UsernameIndex or InviteIndex, so rent funding must not be described as an automatically refundable deposit.
This audit sent no transaction and proves no fresh name claim, invitation registration, reward, or applied spawn.
